isnad_matn.isnad_ar: قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو الْحَسَنِ عَلِيُّ بْنُ مَالِكٍ النَّحْوِيُّ قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْفَضْلِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ الْكَاتِبُ قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا يَمُوتُ بْنُ الْمُزَرَّعِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا عِيسَى بْنُ إِسْمَاعِيلَ قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا الْأَصْمَعِيُّ قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا عِيسَى بْنُ عُمَرَ قَالَ
isnad_matn.matn_ar: كَانَ ذُو الرُّمَّةِ الشَّاعِرُ يَذْهَبُ إِلَى النَّفْيِ فِي الْأَفْعَالِ وَكَانَ رُؤْبَةُ بْنُ الْعَجَّاجِ يَذْهَبُ إِلَى الْإِثْبَاتِ فِيهَا فَاجْتَمَعَا فِي يَوْمٍ مِنْ أَيَّامِهِمَا عِنْدَ بِلَالِ بْنِ أَبِي بُرْدَةَ وَهُوَ وَالِي الْبَصْرَةِ وَبِلَالٌ يَعْرِفُ مَا بَيْنَهُمَا مِنَ الْخِلَافِ فَحَضَّهُمَا عَلَى الْمُنَاظَرَةِ فَقَالَ رُؤْبَةُ وَاللَّهِ مَا يَفْحَصُ طَائِرٌ أُفْحُوصًا وَلَا يُقَرْمِصُ سَبُعٌ قُرْمُوصًا إِلَّا كَانَ ذَلِكَ بِقَضَاءِ اللَّهِ وَقَدَرِهِ فَقَالَ لَهُ ذُو الرُّمَّةِ وَاللَّهِ مَا أَذِنَ اللَّهُ لِلذِّئْبِ أَنْ يَأْخُذَ حَلُوبَةَ عَائِلَةِ عِيَالٍ ضَرَائِكَ فَقَالَ لَهُ رُؤْبَةُ أَفَبِمَشِيئَتِهِ أَخَذَهَا أَمْ بِمَشِيئَةِ اللَّهِ فَقَالَ ذُو الرُّمَّةِ بَلْ بِمَشِيئَتِهِ وَإِرَادَتِهِ فَقَالَ رُؤْبَةُ هَذَا وَاللَّهِ الْكَذِبُ عَلَى الذِّئْبِ فَقَالَ ذُو الرُّمَّةِ وَاللَّهِ الْكَذِبُ عَلَى الذِّئْبِ أَهْوَنُ مِنَ الْكَذِبِ عَلَى رَبِّ الذِّئْبِ فَقَالَ وَأَنْشَدَنِي أَبُو الْحَسَنِ عَلِيُّ بْنُ مَالِكٍ النَّحْوِيُّ فِي أَثَرِ هَذَا الْحَدِيثِ لِمَحْمُودٍ الْوَرَّاقِ أَعَاذِلُ لَمْ آتِ الذُّنُوبَ عَلَى جَهْلٍ وَلَا أَنَّهَا مِنْ فِعْلِ غَيْرِي وَلَا فِعْلِي وَلَا جُرْأَةً مِنِّي عَلَى اللَّهِ جِئْتُهَا وَلَا أَنَّ جَهْلِي لَا يُحِيطُ بِهِ عَقْلِي وَلَكِنْ يُحْسِنُ الظَّنَّ مِنِّي بِعَفْوِ مَنْ تَفَرَّدَ بِالصُّنْعِ الْجَمِيلِ وَبِالْفَضْلِ فَإِنْ صَدَقَ الظَّنُّ الَّذِي قَدْ ظَنَنْتُهُ فَفِي فَضْلِهِ مَا صَدَقَ الظَّنَّ مِنْ مِثْلِي وَإِنْ نَالَنِي مِنْهُ الْعِقَابُ فَإِنَّمَا أَتَيْتُ مِنَ الْإِنْصَافِ فِي الْحُكْمِ وَالْعَدْلِ

narrators.name_ar: أَبُو الْحَسَنِ عَلِيُّ بْنُ مَالِكٍ النَّحْوِيُّ

narrators.known_identity: Abu Abdillah Muhammad ibn Ahmad ibn Ibrahim al-Katib, a scribe and hadith transmitter
Yamut ibn al-Muzarra' (d. 304 AH), scholar of literature, nephew of al-Jahiz

narrators.ambiguity_note: Common name; identified by chain context

narrators.known_identity: Abu Sa'id Abd al-Malik ibn Qurayb al-Asma'i (d. 216 AH), famous Arab philologist
Isa ibn Umar al-Thaqafi (d. 149 AH), famous Basran grammarian

en.summary: This narration records a celebrated theological debate between two famous Umayyad-era Arab poets: Dhu al-Rummah, who denied divine intervention in human acts, and Ru'bah ibn al-Ajjaj, who affirmed it. Their exchange took place before the governor of Basrah, using vivid animal metaphors to argue about God's role in worldly events. It concludes with poetry by Mahmud al-Warraq that takes a middle path: acknowledging personal responsibility for sins while placing hope in God's unique capacity for forgiveness and justice.

key_terms.النفي: Negation -- denying divine predestination of human acts
key_terms.الاثبات: Affirmation -- affirming divine decree over all acts

en.seo_question: What is the debate between Dhu al-Rummah and Ru'bah ibn al-Ajjaj about free will and divine decree?

zh.summary: 两位著名诗人关于神圣干预的神学辩论。
zh.seo_question: 祖·鲁玛和鲁巴关于自由意志和神圣命定的辩论是什么？

translations.en: He said: Abu al-Hasan Ali ibn Malik al-Nahwi narrated to us from Muhammad ibn al-Fadhl, from Abu Abdillah Muhammad ibn Ahmad ibn Ibrahim al-Katib, from Yamut ibn al-Muzarra', from Isa ibn Ismail, from al-Asma'i, from Isa ibn Umar, saying:
translations.ur: انہوں نے کہا: ہم سے ابوالحسن علی بن مالک نحوی نے بیان کیا... عیسیٰ بن عمر نے بیان کیا:
translations.tr: Dedi: Bize Ebu'l-Hasan Ali b. Mâlik en-Nahvî anlattı... İsa b. Ömer anlattı:
translations.fa: گفت: ابوالحسن نحوی نقل کرد... عیسی بن عمر نقل کرد:
translations.id: Dia berkata: Abu al-Hasan al-Nahwi menceritakan... Isa bin Umar menceritakan:
translations.bn: তিনি বলেছেন: আবুল হাসান নাহবী বর্ণনা করেছেন... ঈসা ইবনে উমার বর্ণনা করেছেন:
translations.es: Dijo: Nos narró Abu al-Hasan al-Nahwi... Nos narró Isa ibn Umar:
translations.fr: Il dit : Abu al-Hasan al-Nahwi nous a rapporté... Isa ibn Umar nous a rapporté :
translations.de: Er sagte: Abu al-Hasan al-Nahwi berichtete uns... Isa ibn Umar berichtete uns:
translations.ru: Он сказал: Абу аль-Хасан ан-Нахви рассказал нам... Иса ибн Умар рассказал нам:
translations.zh: 他说：阿布·哈桑纳赫维告诉我们…伊萨·本·欧麦尔告诉我们说：

translations.en: Dhu al-Rummah, the poet, held the view of negation regarding divine intervention in human acts, while Ru'bah ibn al-Ajjaj held the view of affirmation. One day, they met at Bilal ibn Abi Burdah's court, the governor of Basrah. Bilal urged them to debate. Ru'bah said: 'By God, a bird does not dig its nesting place, nor does a predator find its den, except by the decree and destiny of God.' Dhu al-Rummah replied: 'By God, God did not permit the wolf to take the milch-animal of a poor, afflicted family.' Ru'bah said: 'Did the wolf take it by its own will, or by the will of God?' Dhu al-Rummah said: 'Rather, by its own will and desire.' Ru'bah said: 'This, by God, is a lie against the wolf!' Dhu al-Rummah said: 'By God, a lie against the wolf is lesser than a lie against the Lord of the wolf.'
translations.ur: ذوالرمہ شاعر افعال میں نفی کا قائل تھا اور رؤبہ اثبات کا۔ بلال کے پاس مناظرہ ہوا۔ رؤبہ: 'اللہ کی قسم کوئی پرندہ گھونسلا نہیں کھودتا مگر قضا و قدر سے۔' ذوالرمہ: 'اللہ نے بھیڑیے کو اجازت نہیں دی۔' رؤبہ: 'اپنی مرضی سے یا اللہ کی مشیت سے؟' ذوالرمہ: 'اپنی مرضی سے۔' رؤبہ: 'بھیڑیے پر جھوٹ!' ذوالرمہ: 'بھیڑیے پر جھوٹ رب پر جھوٹ سے آسان ہے۔'
translations.tr: Zü'r-Rumme ilahi müdahaleyi reddetme, Rübe ispat görüşündeydi. Basra valisi Bilal'in yanında münazara ettiler...
translations.fa: ذوالرمّه قائل به نفی بود و رؤبه قائل به اثبات. نزد بلال والی بصره مناظره کردند...
translations.id: Dzu al-Rummah menafikan, Ru'bah menegaskannya. Di hadapan Bilal gubernur Basrah berdebat...
translations.bn: যু আর-রুম্মাহ ঐশ্বরিক হস্তক্ষেপ অস্বীকার করতেন, রুবাহ পক্ষে...
translations.es: Dhu al-Rummah negaba la intervención divina; Ru'bah la afirmaba...
translations.fr: Dhu al-Rummah soutenait la négation, Ru'bah l'affirmation...
translations.de: Dhu al-Rummah vertrat die Verneinung, Ru'bah die Bejahung...
translations.zh: 诗人祖·鲁玛主张否认神圣干预，鲁巴主张肯定…

translations.en: Then he said: And Abu al-Hasan Ali ibn Malik al-Nahwi recited to me verses by Mahmud al-Warraq: 'O censurer, I did not commit sins out of ignorance, nor were they another's doing, nor mine; nor out of insolence toward God, nor because my ignorance overcame my intellect; but I hold favourable expectation of the forgiveness of the One peerless in gracious deeds and bounty. If my expectation proves true, in His bounty is confirmation for one like me; and if punishment befalls me, it came by way of fairness in judgment and justice.'
translations.ur: پھر محمود وراق کے اشعار: 'اے ملامت کرنے والے، گناہ جہالت سے نہیں کیے، بلکہ معافی پر حسن ظن سے۔'
translations.tr: Mahmud el-Verrak'ın şiiri: 'Ey kınayıcı, cehaletle değil, güzel işlerde eşsiz olanın affına güzel zanla yaptım.'
translations.fa: اشعار محمود ورّاق: 'ای سرزنشگر، از جهل نیاوردم، بلکه به بخشش یکتا حسن ظن دارم.'
translations.id: Syair Mahmud al-Warraq: 'Wahai pencela, bukan kebodohan, tetapi berbaik sangka pada ampunan Yang tiada bandingan.'
translations.bn: মাহমুদ আল-ওয়াররাকের কবিতা: 'হে নিন্দুক, অজ্ঞতাবশত নয়, বরং অদ্বিতীয় সত্তার ক্ষমায় সুধারণায়ে।'
translations.es: Versos de Mahmud al-Warraq: 'No por ignorancia ni insolencia, sino con buena expectativa del perdón de Aquel sin par.'
translations.fr: Vers de Mahmud al-Warraq : 'Pas par ignorance ni insolence, mais avec bonne attente du pardon de Celui sans égal.'
translations.de: Verse von Mahmud al-Warraq: 'Nicht aus Unwissenheit, sondern mit guter Erwartung an Vergebung des Einzigartigen.'
translations.ru: Стихи Махмуда аль-Варрака: 'Не по невежеству, но с благоприятным ожиданием прощения Того, Кто единственен.'
translations.zh: 马哈穆德·瓦拉克的诗：'并非无知，而是怀着对独一无二者宽恕的美好期望。'

key_phrases.phrase_en: by the decree and destiny of God
a lie against the Lord of the wolf

similar_content_hints.description: Narrations in Usul al-Kafi discussing the intermediate position (al-amr bayn al-amrayn) between absolute determinism and absolute free will.
The statement attributed to Imam al-Sadiq: 'Neither absolute compulsion nor absolute delegation, but a matter between two matters.'
Poetry and debates about divine justice and human responsibility in classical Arabic adab literature.
